narratives.evidence_quote: दुनिया जंग के ढेर पर बैठी है. ... कुल मिलाकर दुनिया बारूद के ढेर पर बैठी है.
narratives.reasoning: The text repeatedly uses hyperbolic and alarming language ('sitting on a heap of war', 'sitting on a heap of gunpowder') to evoke fear and anxiety about widespread global conflict, directly promoting a narrative of imminent danger.

narratives.evidence_quote: उससे लग रहा है कि इजरायल, मिडिल ईस्ट का नया बादशाह बन सकता है.
narratives.reasoning: The text makes a speculative prediction about a potential future outcome of the conflict, stating that Israel 'could become the new emperor of the Middle East', which fits the definition of speculating on war results.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: Russia will also attack other countries
subnarratives.evidence_quote: नाटो की फौजें रूस को चुनौती दे रही हैं.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement frames NATO forces as actively challenging Russia, implying a direct military confrontation that could escalate beyond Ukraine, aligning with the fear that Russia's aggression may extend to other nations.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: दुनिया बारूद के ढेर पर बैठी है.
subnarratives.reasoning: This phrase amplifies war-related fears by painting a global picture of extreme tension and imminent danger, supporting the parent narrative but not fitting any of the more specific subnarrative definitions provided.
